name: BQ24075RGTR
features: Dynamic power management (DPM) limits USB input current
Battery supplement mode -- system can draw more than USB current limit
Power good and charge status outputs
summary: Advanced power path management — seamless USB/battery switching
designNotes: Premium option -- power path management allows USB and battery simultaneously
SYS output stays powered from USB when battery is absent -- good for development
DPM prevents overloading USB port when charging and operating keyboard at same time
QFN-16 package requires reflow soldering -- not hand-solderable
Most complex charger option but best power management
Recommended when using Holyiot 18010 or bare nRF52840 in designs needing robust power
Requires more external components than MCP73831 or TP4056
TS pin needs 10k NTC or 10k/68.1k voltage divider to disable temperature monitoring
concerns: QFN-16 package requires reflow soldering — not hand-solderable
Most complex charger option with many external components
Higher cost than MCP73831 or TP4056
TS pin needs NTC thermistor or resistor divider to disable temp monitoring
Overkill for simple keyboard builds with small batteries
